sections.title: Chief Complaint
sections.description: Primary presenting complaint in the patient's own words, with duration when stated. Keep to a single brief, objective phrase; capture laterality or site only when the patient states it.

sections.title: History of Present Illness
sections.description: Onset, duration, character, location, radiation, severity (capture any numeric scale or descriptor as stated), and timing; aggravating and relieving factors; associated symptoms (fever, weight change, fatigue, etc.); and management or treatment tried to date — each captured only as the patient or physician states it. Favour brief, objective phrasing. Emit one claim per distinct element.

sections.title: Past Medical History
sections.description: Relevant active or past medical conditions as stated (e.g. hypertension, type 2 diabetes, asthma), with duration or control where given. Capture only what is mentioned; leave not_captured if none discussed.

sections.title: Medications
sections.description: Current medications with dose, route, and frequency exactly as stated (e.g. 'metformin 500 mg orally twice daily'); include relevant PRN or over-the-counter agents. Leave not_captured if none discussed.

sections.title: Allergies
sections.description: Stated drug, food, or material allergies and the reaction when given. Leave not_captured if allergies are not addressed during the encounter.

sections.title: Physical Examination
sections.description: Objective, measurable physical findings as observed. Capture vitals with units exactly as stated (e.g. blood pressure in mmHg, heart rate in bpm, temperature in degrees, oxygen saturation as a percentage); document inspection, palpation, auscultation, and any system-specific findings by region or organ system, leading with the structure examined. Terse, telegraphic, objective phrasing is appropriate; one claim per distinct finding, described only as the physician states it.

sections.title: Assessment
sections.description: The physician's working assessment. When synthesis is permitted (grounded mode), present it as one claim per working problem plus any contributing factors the physician relates, and ground each statement in the history/exam/vitals source(s) it rests on (cite multiple sources where relevant). Never state an assessment the cited findings do not support; never add a problem the physician did not voice.

sections.title: Plan
sections.description: The plan as stated by the physician, with one claim per item grouped by: investigations ordered, treatment started or adjusted (with dose/frequency where given), referrals, advice given, and follow-up timing.
